title: centaurea × flahaultii
intro: Centaurea × flahaultii, also known as Flahault's cornflower, is a hybrid plant derived from the Cornflower (Centaurea cyanus) and is native to Europe. This plant contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which have been reported to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Centaurea × flahaultii include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and promoting relaxation. Herbal preparations of this plant can be consumed as a tea, used in infusions, or applied topically as an ointment, although it's essential to use caution and consult with a healthcare professional before using it medicinally.
